channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK Defense Secretary Resigns, Warns Military Lacks Resources for Rising Threats
item.description: John Healey resigned from Prime Minister Keir Starmer's government, accusing it of failing to adequately fund the military amid growing global threats. The resignation raises concerns about NATO burden-sharing as the US considers cutting its European presence.

item.title: 🤖 Tech Executives from OpenAI, Anthropic, Google to Attend G7 Summit
item.description: AI leaders including Sam Altman, Dario Amodei, and Demis Hassabis will join G7 leaders in France next week to discuss AI governance and online safety. The meeting comes as two of the companies prepare for landmark IPOs.

item.title: 🇨🇭 Geneva Steps Up Security for 50,000-Strong Anti-G7 Protest
item.description: Swiss authorities are boarding up businesses and deploying security ahead of a major anti-G7 protest expected this weekend. France hosts the summit next week in neighboring Évian-les-Bains.

item.title: 🛢️ Oil Falls to Near Two-Month Lows as Trump Calls Off Iran Strikes
item.description: Crude prices dropped over 4% after President Trump cancelled new strikes on Iran, reducing fears of escalation. The move signals potential de-escalation in the US-Iran conflict that has disrupted oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: 🇬🇧 Anti-Immigrant Unrest Sparks Violence in Belfast
item.description: Riots and attacks targeting immigrants have spread in east Belfast, with Sudanese families among those targeted. The violence reflects growing tensions over immigration in Northern Ireland.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇪🇺 US to slash fighter jets and warships for NATO operations in Europe
item.description: The US plans to significantly reduce aircraft and warships for NATO in Europe, including cutting F-16 and F-15E fighter jets from about 150 to 100, as Washington shifts focus. The move raises concerns among European allies about US commitment to continental defense.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇪🇺 China cancels high-level diplomatic meetings with EU at short notice
item.description: Beijing abruptly cancelled two diplomatic meetings with the European Union planned for June, Financial Times reports. The move signals growing tensions between China and Europe amid trade disputes and geopolitical friction.

item.title: 🇮🇷⚔️ Iran conflict leaves world in dangerous limbo despite cease-fire
item.description: Two months after a nominal cease-fire, Iran, Israel and the US remain locked in low-intensity conflict. Neither peace nor full war, the situation creates ongoing uncertainty for oil markets and regional stability.

item.title: 🌍💰 G7 summit to spotlight global economic imbalances as China exports surge
item.description: France uses its G7 presidency to highlight mounting global trade tensions driven by China export surge, chronic US deficits, and weak European investment. The summit in Evian comes as world economy faces financial shock risks.

item.title: 🇩🇪⚔️ German Army Chief Warns Russia Could Attack NATO by 2029
item.description: German army chief Lt. Gen. Carsten Brecht warned that Russia will be prepared to attack NATO by 2029, citing "NATO-agreed intelligence." The warning adds to growing Western concerns about Russian military buildup near alliance borders.

item.title: 🇮🇷🤝 South Asian and Gulf Leaders Intervened to Stop Trump Striking Iran
item.description: A new report reveals South Asian and Gulf leaders raced to present Trump with a diplomatic alternative to military strikes on Iran, presenting him with a deal framework. The diplomatic intervention came as Trump had signaled imminent attacks.

item.title: 🇮🇱🇵🇸 Israel Releases Hamas Co-Founder Hassan Yousef After Two Years Detention
item.description: Israel has released Hamas co-founder Hassan Yousef after more than two years of detention. Yousef is a senior leader of Hamas in the West Bank, having co-founded the group in 1987 along with Sheikh Ahmed Yassin.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇺🇸 China Confirms Arrest of US Citizen Suspected of Spying
item.description: China has confirmed the arrest of a US citizen suspected of espionage activities. The arrest adds to growing tensions between Beijing and Washington amid ongoing disputes over trade, technology, and regional security.

item.title: 🦠 Ebola Outbreak Spreads in Congo as Cracked Coffin Traced to Super-Spreader Event
item.description: A pastor's coffin that broke on the rocky road to his burial is suspected as one of the earliest super-spreader events in Congo's growing Ebola epidemic.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine and Russia Exchange Overnight Drone Strikes, Targeting Energy and Rail Systems
item.description: Russia and Ukraine carried out far-reaching drone strikes overnight, with Ukraine hitting a major oil processing region while Russia targeted railway stations and electrical substations.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran war deal close as Strait of Hormuz tensions linger
item.description: Hopes for peace grew after Trump said a deal could be signed this weekend, though Tehran said no final decision has been made on a pact. The strategic Strait of Hormuz remains a key sticking point in negotiations.

item.title: ✈️ US and EU near deadline on $11.5 billion tariffs in decades-long aircraft dispute
item.description: Washington and Brussels have yet to decide whether to continue suspending or reimpose tariffs on $11.5 billion of goods in a dispute over aircraft subsidies, with their truce expiring within days.

item.title: 🇵🇭 Philippines Defense Chief Vows to Press on Despite Chinese Sanctions
item.description: Philippine Defense Secretary Gilberto Teodoro vowed to continue his duties despite China sanctioning him for "erroneous remarks," calling out China's "wickedness" as tensions escalate over South China Sea disputes.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK Defense Minister Resigns Over Defense Funding
item.description: UK Armed Forces Minister Al Carns resigned in protest over lack of defense funding, following Defense Secretary John Healey's earlier resignation. Prime Minister Keir Starmer faces a political crisis as two defense ministers quit within days.

item.title: 🇦🇪 UAE Sends Military Planes to South Korea for Missile Defense Transport
item.description: The United Arab Emirates dispatched military aircraft to South Korea to expedite transport of Korean-made Cheongung missile defense systems, highlighting deepening defense cooperation.

item.title: 🇪🇺 ECB signals option to raise rates again in July after insurance hike
item.description: The European Central Bank raised interest rates for the first time in nearly three years, with Bundesbank President Nagel saying all options remain open for July to combat Iran-war-driven energy inflation.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK lags European rivals in curbing influx of cheap parcels from China
item.description: More cheap parcels are flowing into the UK from China than into rival European economies, undermining British retailers and raising concerns about the UKs ability to address the trade imbalance.

item.title: ♟️ World chess body suspends Russia after sports tribunal ruling
item.description: The International Chess Federation suspended Russia following a Court of Arbitration for Sport ruling in favor of Ukraine, ending decades of Russian dominance in the game.

item.title: 🇵🇭 Disaster drills helped prevent more deaths in powerful Philippine earthquake
item.description: Philippine officials say years of disaster-preparedness drills helped prevent a larger casualty toll when a powerful magnitude 7.8 earthquake struck the southern Philippines.

item.title: ⛽🇺🇦 Fuel Stations in Crimea Run Dry After Ukrainian Drone Strikes
item.description: Fuel stations on the Russian-held Crimean peninsula were out of petrol on Thursday as a Ukrainian campaign against supply lines escalates, highlighting the effectiveness of Ukrainian strikes on Russian logistics.
